assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 16: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' — identical to claude-md-configured.md line 16.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 17: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — new row with serena_ui as Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 20-26 contain identical Jira Configuration to claude-md-configured.md lines 19-25: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has '## Added' section listing trustify-ui row and serena_ui limitation entry, and '## Preserved (Unchanged)' section listing trustify-backend row, all 5 Jira Configuration fields, Code Intelligence convention/example/serena_backend limitation, and all 3 Bug Configuration fields.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 2 lines 31-32: '1. **serena_backend** — already in Repository Registry (10 tools detected)' and '2. **serena_ui** — NEW, not in Repository Registry (10 tools detected)'.

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 28-42 preserve the naming convention ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name'), the serena_backend find_symbol example, and the serena_backend limitation ('rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use'). Only line 43 ('`serena_ui`: No known limitations') was added.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 45-49 contain identical Bug Configuration to claude-md-configured.md lines 44-48: Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks. discovery-log.md Step 9: 'Bug Configuration is up to date — all three required fields are already populated. Skipped.'

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10: 'User declined to enable security triage. Security Configuration not scaffolded.' changes-log.md: 'Not scaffolded: user declined to enable security triage'. claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section (file ends at line 50 after Bug Configuration).
